data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Spotify uses a microservices architecture to manage its vast array of features and services independently.
data.technical_spec: Each service is maintained independently, with APIs for communication.

data.title: APIs and SDKs
data.description: APIs and SDKs enable developers to integrate Spotify's functionalities into their applications.
data.technical_spec: RESTful APIs, Web APIs, and mobile SDKs available for integration.

data.title: Cloud Infrastructure
data.description: Spotify leverages cloud infrastructure for scalability and resilience.
data.technical_spec: Uses Google Cloud Platform to handle storage, processing, and distribution.

data.title: Data Management
data.description: Data from users and streams is managed and analyzed in real-time.
data.technical_spec: BigQuery and Hadoop used for data processing and analytics.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: CDNs are employed to ensure fast and efficient delivery of media content globally.
data.technical_spec: CDN providers like Akamai and Cloudflare are used to cache and distribute content.
